pages.text:
WHEREAS It 1a desired to make provlaion with regard to the places of burial ia French territory of membere of the Armed Forces of the Coumonmealth countries who have fallen in the wars of 1914-18 and 1939-45 and have been interred in French territory.

AND WHEREAS an Agreement to these enda has beet drawn up betteen the Goverrment of the Republic of

France on the one hand and the Goreromenta of the

Commonwealth countries on the other hand.

AND WEREAS It is expedient that a fit person should be invested with full porera to sign the said Agreement in socordance mith ita provisions for the Government of New Zealand

I, THOMAS OLIFTON WEBB, Minlater of External Affeire of NeT Zealand, DO HEREBY CERTIFY THAT

JANE ROBERTSON MOKENZIE, New Zealand Charge d'Affaires at paris is, by these presents named, conatituted and appolnted to sign the said Agreement for the

Goverment of Not Zealand.

And I do hereby cortley that the said agreement, when signed, shall be acknowledged and accepted by the

Government of New Zealand In the fullest manner.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF I have signed these presents and affized hereto the aeal of the Minister of External

Affaira of Her Zealand.

SIGNED AND SEALED at Welliagton this fifth day of

October

1951.

Minister of External Affairs.
